title.en: Late June at Mt Rainier is the happiest place near Seattle.
title.zh_cn: 六月底的雷尼尔山是西雅图最快乐的地方！

description.en: Don't skip Mt Rainier when you're in Seattle — it looms right behind the city. In late June the snow hasn't melted yet, so after the climb you can slide happily back down the slope. Highly recommend the scenic Skyline Trail.
description.zh_cn: 来西雅图千万别忘了去它背后的雷尼尔山耍一耍。六月中下旬雪还没化，爬上去之后可以开心地一路滑下山。强烈推荐这条风景绝佳的天际线步道（Skyline Trail）。

title.en: A cold, secluded end-of-the-world coastline on Vancouver Island.
title.zh_cn: 世界尽头的冷酷秘境，温哥华岛的自闭海岸。

description.en: A record of hiking Vancouver Island's south coast last June, through Sooke Coast Regional Park and Botanical Beach. This stretch of coastline is strikingly cold and remote, unlike anywhere else on the island. Tide pools, jagged rock, and rolling fog make it feel like the edge of the world.
description.zh_cn: 这是去年六月在温哥华岛南部徒步的记录，主要走了苏克海岸和植物学沙滩两片区域。这片海岸线格外冷峻孤绝，和岛上其他地方的风光很不一样。潮池、礁石和浓雾让人有种走到世界尽头的错觉。

title.en: A preview: the Mt Rainier looming behind Seattle is stunning.
title.zh_cn: 预告：西雅图身后的雷尼尔山美得让人震惊！

description.en: A short preview shot around Mt Rainier, with ground footage from around Tipsoo Lake. The drone shots were captured from Chinook Pass, just outside the national park boundary, since flying inside the park itself isn't allowed. The next day was spent hiking the Skyline Trail partway up the mountain.
description.zh_cn: 这是一段雷尼尔山的预告短片，地面画面拍摄于蒂普索湖（Tipsoo Lake）周边。航拍画面则是在国家公园边界之外的钦努克山口（Chinook Pass）拍摄的，因为公园内部禁止飞无人机。第二天还去走了雷尼尔山半山腰的天际线步道（Skyline Trail）。

title.en: Deep in the lair of Utah's "goblins."
title.zh_cn: 深入美国犹他州"哥布林"的老巢。

description.en: Goblin Valley sits near Hanksville, Utah, and it's one of the more whimsical stops in the state. The valley is packed with rounded, oddly cute little hoodoos that look like a crowd of goblins from a distance. It's an easy, playful detour on any Utah road trip.
description.zh_cn: 哥布林山谷（Goblin Valley）位于犹他州汉克斯维尔（Hanksville）附近，是一处非常有趣的地貌。山谷里布满了圆润又丑萌的小石头，远远看去就像一群哥布林。这也是本次犹他之行中最好玩的一站之一。

title.en: Welcome to the Space Needle — the most alien landscape in America.
title.zh_cn: 欢迎来到太空针，美国最像外星的地方。

description.en: This episode covers three otherworldly formations near Hanksville, Utah: the Space Needle, Moon Overlook, and Factory Butte. All three sit close together along the same stretch of cliffs. They were all carved by the same cycle of bone-dry desert and brief, violent flash floods.
description.zh_cn: 这一期分享犹他州汉克斯维尔附近的三处外星地貌：太空针、月球表面和工厂山。三处景观都集中在同一片悬崖上下，彼此相距不远。它们都是干旱沙漠与短期山洪交替侵蚀雕刻出的产物。

title.en: Utah's most spectacular views all sit right on the edge of a cliff.
title.zh_cn: 犹他州最壮阔的风景，全部都在悬崖之上。

description.en: A record of the Great Goosenecks of the San Juan River, all near the famous "Forrest Gump Point" by Monument Valley. Stops along the way include Mexican Hat, Raplee Ridge, Goosenecks State Park, and the Valley of the Gods overlook. Every one of them is a cliff-edge view down onto the river's tight, winding curves.
description.zh_cn: 这是圣胡安河鹅颈湾（The Great Goosenecks）一带的记录，都在纪念碑谷热门打卡点"阿甘点"附近。沿途经过墨西哥宽边帽、梳子岭、鹅颈湾州立公园、诸神山谷观景台等地。每一处都是站在悬崖边俯瞰河流蜿蜒的绝佳视角。

title.en: This region has plenty of beautiful canyons — just none inside Canyonlands itself.
title.zh_cn: 峡谷地地区好看的峡谷很多，可惜都不在峡谷地里。

description.en: This was supposed to be a day trip centered on Canyonlands National Park, but Potash Road and the Potash Ponds we saw that morning turned out prettier than the park itself. Deadhorse Point State Park, where we caught the sunset, outshone Canyonlands too. The park's own Island in the Sky district is impressive, but honestly less fun and less photogenic than neighboring Arches.
description.zh_cn: 这本该是峡谷地国家公园（Canyonlands）的一日游，结果早上去的钾肥路和钾肥池反而更好看。傍晚看日落的死马点州立公园（Deadhorse Point）同样比峡谷地本身更出片。峡谷地园区内的天空岛（Island in the Sky）震撼归震撼，但论好玩程度还是不如隔壁的拱门国家公园。

title.en: I found the most wonderful Oregon, at the edge of a sea of forest.
title.zh_cn: 我找到了最美妙的俄勒冈，就在森林之海的尽头。

description.en: A three-day road trip down the Oregon coast in late October, hitting twelve landmark stops along the way. Natural Bridges and Heceta Head Lighthouse felt the most quintessentially Oregon, capturing its rocky shoreline and lighthouse scenery respectively. Cape Arago was the biggest surprise, while the golden fog at sunrise over Sisters Rock left the deepest impression.
description.zh_cn: 这是十月底沿俄勒冈海岸线为期三天的自驾之旅，一路走过十二个知名景点。最有俄勒冈气质的当属天然桥（Natural Bridges）和赫西塔角灯塔（Heceta Head Lighthouse），分别代表了礁石海岸和灯塔风光。最大的惊喜是阿拉戈角（Cape Arago），印象最深的则是姐妹岩（Sisters Rock）日出时那片金色雾气。

title.en: Caving in California: I broke into the underworld beneath a volcano.
title.zh_cn: 加州探洞：我闯入了火山下的地心世界。

description.en: This is a record of my very first real caving trip, at an undeveloped lava tube in Northern California called Pluto's Cave. Crawling through it genuinely feels like breaking into a volcanic underworld. The whole thing turned out exciting enough that this episode ran long on its own.
description.zh_cn: 这是人生第一次真正意义上的探洞记录，地点是加州北部一处未开发的野洞——冥王星洞（Pluto's Cave）。这个洞本身是一条古老的熔岩隧道，钻进去之后仿佛真的闯入了地下的火山世界。这次的体验足够刺激有趣，篇幅也因此拉得比较长。

title.en: I waited two years for this one photo.
title.zh_cn: 为了这张照片，我等了整整两年。

description.en: Kasha-Katuwe Tent Rocks National Monument, in northern New Mexico, looks eerily like Turkey's Cappadocia. It only issues 100 tickets a day and was actually closed from 2020 all the way through November 2024, making it brutally hard to get into. The slot canyon inside blew past expectations — the only letdown is how small the whole area is, just a single five-kilometer trail.
description.zh_cn: 帐篷岩国家纪念碑（Tent Rocks）位于新墨西哥州北部，地貌神似土耳其的卡帕多西亚。这里每天只放行100张门票，还从2020年一直封闭到2024年11月，所以格外难约。里面那段狭缝峡谷大大超出预期，唯一的遗憾是面积太小，全程只有一条五公里的徒步线路。

title.en: Only after seeing sunset at White Sands did I know America could be this beautiful.
title.zh_cn: 见过白沙的日落，才知道美国也可以这么美。

description.en: White Sands National Park, in southern New Mexico, might be the most photogenic national park in the country. We went sand-sledding on the Backcountry Camping Trail, looked for animal tracks on the Dune Life Nature Trail, and caught sunset on the Alkali Flat Trail. Arrive three or four hours before sunset, bring sunglasses, sunscreen, and plenty of water, and never wander too deep into the dunes after dark.
description.zh_cn: 白沙国家公园位于新墨西哥州南部，我愿称它为"美国最出片的国家公园"。我们在 Backcountry Camping Trail 滑沙，在 Dune Life Nature Trail 找动物足迹，最后在 Alkali Flat Trail 看日落。建议日落前三四小时抵达，并备好墨镜、防晒和充足饮水，千万别在天黑后还深入沙丘。

title.en: If you're homesick, go stand by the sea at Pacifica.
title.zh_cn: 如果你很想家，就去帕西菲卡看海。

description.en: Pacifica is the closest stretch of ocean to my office, with an unusual black-sand beach and a scenic headland called Mori Point. On February 1, 2024, after nearly three years without a trip home, homesickness got the better of me and I wrote a short poem here. This one's dedicated to everyone living far from home in the SF Bay Area.
description.zh_cn: 帕西菲卡是离我公司最近的一片海，这里有一片有趣的黑沙滩，还有风景不错的莫离角（Mori Point）。2024年2月1日，因为快三年没回国了，实在太想家，于是在这里写下了一首小诗。这段文字献给所有在旧金山湾区的留学生和游子们。

title.en: Spring in the SF Bay Area looks like Switzerland everywhere you look.
title.zh_cn: 旧金山湾区的春天，到处都是小瑞士。

description.en: There's no real story behind Vargas Plateau — just a classic Bay Area rolling-hills scene. Green slopes ripple out toward the horizon in every direction. It's the kind of view that shows up all over the Bay every spring, and never quite gets old.
description.zh_cn: 瓦尔加斯高原（Vargas Plateau）没什么特别的故事，就是一片典型的湾区春季 rolling hills。绿色的山坡起起伏伏，一路延伸到天边。这种画面每年春天在湾区随处可见，却百看不厌。

title.en: Look, Valley of Fire is burning — doesn't it look like your rusted heart?
title.zh_cn: 你看呐，火焰谷在燃烧，像不像你生锈的心。

description.en: Valley of Fire State Park sits just outside Las Vegas, and the whole park is made of blazing red rock. Plenty of visitors think it's easily good enough to be a national park — Nevada just doesn't seem interested in making that case. The best-known feature inside is the "Fire Wave," a rippled slab of red sandstone.
description.zh_cn: 火焰谷州立公园就在拉斯维加斯郊外，整片园区都是红得发烫的石头。很多人都觉得这里的风景完全够格成为国家公园，只是内华达州似乎并不打算申请。其中最出名的一处叫"火浪"（Fire Wave），是一块波浪状的红色岩石。

title.en: Candy Cliffs — an alien-looking "Red Pocket," even wilder than White Pocket.
title.zh_cn: 外星糖果崖，比白口袋更刺激的"红口袋"。

description.en: Yant Flat looks strikingly like the famous White Pocket, but it's just an hour's drive from St. George, Utah. The hike in is only about a kilometer, making it far more accessible than White Pocket. The rock formations aren't quite as intricate, but the colors are more vivid, and since it sits right on a cliff edge, the whole visit feels a lot more thrilling.
description.zh_cn: 杨特坪（Yant Flat）是一处和著名的白口袋（White Pocket）神似的地貌，就在犹他州圣乔治小镇一小时车程外。徒步距离只有一公里多，比白口袋好去太多。石头形态不如白口袋精致，但颜色更艳丽，加上本身是悬崖地形，体验也更刺激。

title.en: I booked a snowstorm and turned Bryce Canyon into a fairy tale.
title.zh_cn: 我预定了一场大雪，把布莱斯峡谷变成了童话世界。

description.en: Bryce Canyon National Park might be the most beautiful national park in America during winter. We hiked the classic, beginner-friendly Navajo Loop into Queen's Garden Trail, starting at Sunset Point and finishing at Sunrise Point. We caught sunrise at Inspiration Point and sunset at Bryce Point — if you're heading to the American West in winter, it's worth timing a snowy day here on purpose.
description.zh_cn: 布莱斯峡谷国家公园大概是美国冬天最好看的国家公园。我们走了最经典也最友好的纳瓦霍环线转皇后花园步道，从日落点出发，最终抵达日出点。日出去了灵感点，日落则选在布莱斯点，冬天去美西的话真的值得专门蹲一个下雪天来这里。

title.en: Hiking Zion National Park in winter — the angels won't be landing.
title.zh_cn: 徒步锡安国家公园：冬天，天使不会降临。

description.en: This time we tried Zion's most famous hike, Angel's Landing. Without the right gear we didn't make it all the way to the top, but the stretch right before the final chained scramble had the best views of the whole trail. Zion is really at its best in fall, when the canyon trees turn gold — we came in winter purely because neighboring Bryce Canyon looks better that season.
description.zh_cn: 这次尝试徒步锡安国家公园最出名的路线——天使降临（Angel's Landing）。因为装备不足，最终没能走到顶端，不过个人觉得转进裂缝之前的那段路景色其实最好。去锡安最好的季节其实是秋天（峡谷里的树会变成金色），这次选冬天纯粹是因为隔壁的布莱斯峡谷冬天更好看。

title.en: 5,000 meters up — Peru's Rainbow Mountain is a wonder of our century.
title.zh_cn: 海拔五千米，秘鲁彩虹山是属于21世纪的奇观。

description.en: Rainbow Mountain is Cusco's newest tourist darling, and it used to be buried entirely under glacial ice. Global warming melted that ice away, exposing the vividly colored mineral layers underneath that give the mountain its name today. What stuck with me most, though, was standing at 5,000 meters — probably the easiest 5,000-meter peak an ordinary person could ever climb.
description.zh_cn: 彩虹山是库斯科周边新晋的网红景点，曾经整座山都被冰川覆盖。全球变暖让冰川融化后，内部色彩艳丽的矿物碎屑才暴露在阳光之下，成了如今的模样。最让人印象深刻的其实是它5000米级的海拔——大概是普通人最容易征服的一座五千米级山峰。

title.en: We found Crescent Lake's long-lost little sister.
title.zh_cn: 我们找到了月牙泉失散多年的亲妹妹。

description.en: The Huacachina oasis, near Ica in southern Peru, looks eerily like China's Crescent Lake in the Mingsha Dunes. The plan was sunset plus a round of sandboarding, but plans didn't survive contact with reality — we only made it in time for the sunset. And for the record, despite having "china" in its name, the oasis has nothing to do with China; it's pure coincidence.
description.zh_cn: 华卡奇纳绿洲位于秘鲁南部伊卡附近，长得和中国的鸣沙山·月牙泉神似。原计划是看日落顺便滑一场沙，结果计划赶不上变化，最后只赶上了日落。顺带一提，这个绿洲名字里虽然带了"china"，但纯属巧合，跟中国毫无关系。

title.en: We're going to find the sea in the middle of the desert.
title.zh_cn: 我们要去沙漠里寻找大海。

description.en: Paracas National Reserve sits about four hours south of Lima, a stretch of coastal desert landscape that looks almost surreal. The desert runs right up to the edge of the ocean here. I came specifically for that half-fire, half-sea shot that ended up as the cover image.
description.zh_cn: 帕拉卡斯国家保护区位于秘鲁首都利马以南四小时车程处，是一片沿海沙漠景观保护区。这里的画面看起来相当奇幻，沙漠一直延伸到海边。这次专程是为了那张一半是火红沙丘、一半是蔚蓝大海的封面画面而来。

title.en: We're going to look for penguins in a tropical desert.
title.zh_cn: 我们要去热带沙漠里寻找企鹅。

description.en: The Ballestas Islands are a cluster of guano-caked rocky islets just off the coast near Paracas, about three and a half hours from Lima. Like the Galápagos, they're home to a surprising variety of unique wildlife — most notably Humboldt penguins. Because it's dramatically cheaper than the real Galápagos, some people abroad call it "the poor man's Galápagos."
description.zh_cn: 巴列斯塔群岛是帕拉卡斯小镇外海的一片礁石群岛，距离利马三个半小时车程，整片岛屿都覆盖着厚厚的鸟粪。这里和加拉帕戈斯群岛一样生活着许多独特的野生动物，其中最特别的就是秘鲁企鹅。因为价格比加拉帕戈斯便宜太多，国外甚至有人称这里为"穷人的加拉帕戈斯"。

title.en: I found the prettiest spot in California — a creek full of hot springs.
title.zh_cn: 我发现了加州最好看的地方，一条有温泉的小溪。

description.en: Hot Creek is a can't-miss stop along Highway 395, California's own "high-plateau highway," though most visitors just snap a photo from the best lookout point and leave. Walking in closer is genuinely worthwhile — it feels like a wild, far-less-regulated pocket version of Yellowstone. That lookout view really is stunning enough that I'd call it the prettiest spot in California.
description.zh_cn: 热水溪是加州"高原公路"395沿线大家都会打卡的一站，很多人只在视角最佳的观景台拍张照就走。其实走进去看更有意思，就像一个限制少得多的野生小黄石。那个最佳观景点是真的好看，我愿称之为加州最美的地方。

title.en: A fantastical natural temple beneath white cliffs, straight out of Zelda.
title.zh_cn: 白崖下的奇幻天然神庙，仿佛走进了塞尔达传说。

description.en: The Stone Columns of Lake Crowley sit right off Highway 395 in California, an otherworldly cluster of natural pillars that looks like ruins from some ancient civilization. They're said to have formed long ago when snowfall settled onto volcanic ash — a lot of people have actually seen them before, on an old Windows screensaver. One last tip: if the trip involves dirt roads, do not rent an Audi.
description.zh_cn: 克罗利湖石柱就在加州395公路旁边，是一片神奇的天然石柱地貌，看上去像某个古代文明的遗迹。据说是很久以前的积雪落在火山灰上凝结形成的，很多人其实是在 Windows 屏保上先见过它。最后提醒一句：如果行程要走土路，真的不建议租奥迪。

title.en: The one landform China doesn't have — Vancouver has it right outside the door.
title.zh_cn: 中国唯一没有的地貌，温哥华出门就是——峡湾。

description.en: This episode covers three months' worth of exploring Howe Sound, the fjord right outside Vancouver's door. It runs from Lighthouse Park near the city, past Horseshoe Bay and Whytecliff, up the Sea to Sky Highway through Porteau Cove and Murrin Park, all the way to Stawamus Chief and the Squamish estuary at the fjord's far end. Everything got cut into one episode since it all sits along the same route — the Stawamus Chief hike alone had enough footage for its own full-length episode later.
description.zh_cn: 这一期记录了在温哥华断断续续待的三个月里，对家门口这条豪湾（Howe Sound）的探索。沿途走过灯塔公园、马蹄湾、怀特崖，一路开上海天公路，经过波特湾和默林公园，直到峡湾尽头的斯塔瓦姆斯酋长岩和斯阔米什三角洲。整条峡湾一次剪完，其中爬酋长岩的部分因为拍得太多，会单独出一期长篇。

title.en: Moraine Lake, the most beautiful lake in all of North America.
title.zh_cn: 总有一天，你会来到北美最美的湖泊，班夫梦莲湖。

description.en: Moraine Lake is the reason I went to Banff, and it lives up to every bit of its reputation. The peaks, the glacier, the lake, the forest — it's about the best combination you'll find anywhere in North America. Lake Louise next door, by comparison, is pretty but fairly ordinary, and honestly less striking than Emerald Lake in neighboring Yoho National Park.
description.zh_cn: 梦莲湖是我去班夫的原因，也的确名不虚传。雪山、冰川、湖水、森林，都是教科书级的组合，很可能是你能在北美找到的最好看的湖。相比之下，隔壁的露易丝湖好看但普通，反倒不如我们后来去的幽鹤国家公园翡翠湖惊艳。

title.en: Before Niagara Falls disappears, remember to go see it.
title.zh_cn: 在尼亚加拉瀑布消失之前，请你记得去看看它。

description.en: Niagara Falls sits right on the US-Canada border, roughly halfway between Toronto and New York City. Water from four of the five Great Lakes passes through here, and the roar is so loud that Indigenous peoples named it "thundering water." This time I cut a short, medium, and long version of the same footage, just to see which pacing actually works best.
description.zh_cn: 尼亚加拉瀑布正好坐落在美国和加拿大的交界处，恰好也在多伦多和纽约的中间。北美五大湖里有四个湖的水都会流经这里，水声浩大如雷，原住民因此称它为"雷神之水"。这次特意剪了短篇、中篇、长篇三个版本，看看哪种叙事节奏效果最好。

title.en: Five of me, all chasing Mt Fuji on the same day.
title.zh_cn: 同一天，有五个格林都要去找富士山。

description.en: A day trip out of Tokyo to see Mt Fuji from both Lake Yamanaka and Lake Kawaguchi. Hirano-no-hama comes highly recommended — feeding the swans there in the morning was so charming it got its own dedicated episode later. This one experiments with retelling a day that happened in time order as if it happened in spatial order instead.
description.zh_cn: 从东京出发，去山中湖和河口湖两地遥望富士山的一天记录。强烈推荐平野之滨，早上去喂天鹅真的太治愈太有趣了，有趣到后来单独出了一期。这次尝试把原本按时间顺序发生的事，改成按空间顺序来讲述。

title.en: Give the Grand Canyon back to the purple night, in trade for a cute little moon.
title.zh_cn: 把大峡谷还给紫色的夜晚，换一只可爱的月亮。

description.en: The Grand Canyon is a colossal desert gorge in Arizona — the geology textbook back in middle school called it a living record of geologic time, layer by layer. This time around there wasn't enough time on the trip to properly explore it. The only thing we managed was one sunset from Navajo Point on the canyon's South Rim.
description.zh_cn: 科罗拉多大峡谷是亚利桑那州的一座巨型沙漠峡谷，中学地理课本上说它是一本活的地质教科书，一层又一层岩层记录了一年又一年的地质历史。可惜这次美西之行时间紧张，没能好好游览。最后只赶上在大峡谷国家公园南缘的纳瓦霍点看了一场日落。

title.en: One of a kind — a white alien world rarer than the Grand Canyon in your textbook.
title.zh_cn: 全球唯一，比课本里的大峡谷更稀有的白色外星世界。

description.en: White Pocket is a little-known gem near Page, Arizona, just a bit north of the famous Grand Canyon. Its swirling, brain-like white rock formations are so unusual that nowhere else on Earth has anything quite like it, outside this one corner of the Colorado Plateau near the Arizona-Utah border. The road in is rough, but it's absolutely worth it — this genuinely feels like an alien world.
description.zh_cn: 白口袋是亚利桑那佩奇附近的一处小众秘境，就在大名鼎鼎的科罗拉多大峡谷北边不远处。它那种白色脑纹状的岩石地貌极为罕见，除了亚利桑那和犹他交界的这片科罗拉多高原附近，全世界再也找不到第二处。去程的路很不好走，但绝对值得——这里就是真正意义上的外星世界。

title.en: Is Antelope Canyon just Photoshop — is it really that smooth?
title.zh_cn: 羚羊谷是照骗吗？真的比巧克力还丝滑？

description.en: Antelope Canyon X is the uppermost of the six developed sections of the famous Antelope Canyon system, sitting between Page and Monument Valley. The land belongs to the Navajo Nation, so a guided tour is mandatory. I'd always assumed those glowing photos were too good to be true — turns out they're not, and this is an absolute must-visit on any American Southwest trip.
description.zh_cn: X羚羊谷是著名的羚羊谷景区六段中最靠上游的一段，位于佩奇和纪念碑谷之间。这里是纳瓦霍人的私人领地，必须跟团请导游才能进入。去之前一直以为那些照片是"照骗"，实地看过才发现是真的——羚羊谷绝对是美西旅行必去的一站。

title.en: The free, no-permit alternative to the Wave — the New Wave.
title.zh_cn: 波浪谷的平替，免费无需抽签的新波浪谷。

description.en: The New Wave sits near Page, Arizona, a cluster of pink sandstone rippled into wave-like patterns. It's the free, walk-in alternative to the famous Wave, which caps visitors at 64 a day through a permit lottery. The texture isn't quite as fine as the original, but it still makes for genuinely great photos.
description.zh_cn: 新波浪谷位于亚利桑那佩奇附近，是一片带有波浪纹理的粉红色石头群。它算是著名波浪谷（每天限流64人、需要抽签才能进入）的免费开放平替。纹理没有波浪谷那么细腻，但依然能拍出很出片的照片。

title.en: World-famous Monument Valley — what exactly is it a monument to?
title.zh_cn: 举世闻名的纪念碑谷，到底纪念的是什么？

description.en: Monument Valley straddles the Arizona-Utah border, its core area a tribal park sitting on Navajo Nation land. It's an essential stop on any American Southwest trip and shows up constantly in films and TV shows. It's also the direct inspiration for the puzzle game of the same name.
description.zh_cn: 纪念碑谷位于亚利桑那州和犹他州交界处，核心区域是坐落在纳瓦霍原住民保留地上的部落公园。作为美西旅行的必去之地，它频繁出现在各类电影和电视剧中。它也是同名游戏《纪念碑谷》的灵感来源。

title.en: An alien landscape covered in stone mushrooms — yet they call it badlands.
title.zh_cn: 长满岩石蘑菇的外星地貌，却被称为"恶地"。

description.en: The Ah-Shi-Sle-Pah Wilderness sits in northwestern New Mexico, best known for its strange "badlands" terrain. Years of running water have carved the mineral-rich mudstone here into fields of mushroom-shaped hoodoos. The whole area is quiet and desolate enough to feel like stepping onto another planet.
description.zh_cn: 灰盐荒野位于新墨西哥州西北部，最出名的是这里神奇的"恶地"（Badlands）地貌。流水多年冲刷富含矿物质的泥土岩石，把它们雕刻成了一朵朵蘑菇的形状。整片区域安静又荒凉，走在其中像是踏入了另一个星球。

title.en: New Mexico's "Great Wall" ruin looks like a dragon from above.
title.zh_cn: 新墨西哥的"长城"遗迹，高空俯瞰宛如巨龙。

description.en: Shiprock is a striking, dragon-shaped rock formation in northwestern New Mexico. Up close its ridges look almost like brickwork, but it's actually solidified magma that once filled a crack deep underground, only exposed after eons of erosion stripped away everything around it. The local Navajo people consider it sacred ground.
description.zh_cn: 船首岩位于新墨西哥州西北部，是一块形似巨龙的醒目岩石。远看像是人工砌成的砖墙，实际上是白垩纪末期岩浆侵入地表缝隙后凝固形成的岩墙，原本深埋地下，经过漫长的风化侵蚀才裸露出来。当地纳瓦霍原住民将这里奉为圣地。

title.en: San Francisco has its own Scottish Highlands, called Point Reyes.
title.zh_cn: 旧金山有自己的苏格兰高地，叫雷耶斯角。

description.en: Point Reyes is a massive triangular peninsula about an hour and a half north of San Francisco. Its southern bay, Drakes Bay, is ringed by iconic white cliffs and beaches where wild elephant seals sometimes haul out, supposedly named after English explorer Francis Drake's 1576 visit. The headland itself, with its wild grass and low golden light, feels a lot like Scotland's own Highlands.
description.zh_cn: 雷耶斯角是旧金山以北一个半小时车程的一座巨大三角形半岛。南边的德雷克湾有一圈标志性的白崖，沙滩上偶尔能看到野生的象海豹，据说英国航海家弗朗西斯·德雷克曾在1576年到访此地。雷耶斯角本身是一片牛角形的海边高地，荒草和斜阳的气质和苏格兰高地颇有几分神似。

title.en: A day in Monterey, from sunset at Carmel Beach to a starlit aquarium.
title.zh_cn: 蒙特雷的一天：从卡梅尔的日落，到水族馆的海底星空。

description.en: This one experiments with telling the day in reverse, starting from sunset. Monterey Bay Aquarium is the top-ranked aquarium in the US, showcasing a huge range of marine life unique to California, while Carmel-by-the-Sea just south of it is a storybook art town with a gorgeous sunset beach. The 17 Mile Drive scenic road connecting the two is well-known, but after seeing enough of California's coastline, it honestly reads as fairly average.
description.zh_cn: 这次尝试了一次倒叙实验，把一天的行程从日落讲起。蒙特雷水族馆是美国排名第一的水族馆，展示了大量加州特有的海洋生物；南边的卡梅尔小镇则是一座充满童话气息的艺术小镇，拥有一片绝美的日落海滩。两地之间的17英里景观公路很出名，但看多了加州海岸线之后，只能算中等水平。

title.en: California's second-prettiest lighthouse sits just south of San Francisco.
title.zh_cn: 加州第二好看的灯塔，就在旧金山南边。

description.en: Pigeon Point Lighthouse is widely considered California's second-prettiest lighthouse, and one of its tallest — the prettiest is probably Point Arena Lighthouse further north. It sits between San Francisco and Santa Cruz, right along the classic Highway 1 route, though it's unfortunately been closed for repairs since 2021 with no climbing the tower. The rocky beach below turned out to be a tide-pooling hotspot, with plenty of people hauling away full buckets of shellfish.
description.zh_cn: 鸽点灯塔是加州海岸线上公认第二好看的灯塔，也是最高的灯塔之一，第一好看的可能是阿里纳角灯塔。它位于旧金山和圣塔克鲁斯之间，是加州一号公路经典路线上大概率会经过的一站，只可惜从2021年起就因维修而不对外开放上塔。这里同时也是个赶海圣地，石滩上到处都是满载而归的赶海人。

title.en: Highway 1 in the Bay Area hides a cinematic "white cliff."
title.zh_cn: 湾区的加州一号公路下，藏着一片电影感"白崖"。

description.en: I stumbled onto a beach at San Gregorio that was perfect for flying a drone, and ended up trying every camera move I could think of. The cliff itself is actually yellow up close, only reading as white from a distance — nowhere near as pale as England's White Cliffs of Dover. The beach was nearly empty, and the combination of cliff, surf, and sand made for a wonderfully clean, photogenic scene.
description.zh_cn: 意外在圣格雷戈里奥发现了一片很适合放飞无人机的沙滩，忍不住试了各种不同的运镜方式。这片悬崖近看其实是黄色的，只有远观才泛白，没有英国多佛白崖那么纯白。沙滩上几乎没什么人，悬崖、海浪和沙子构成的画面干净又出片。

title.en: The Marin Headlands will never let you down on the Golden Gate Bridge.
title.zh_cn: 海事岬永远不会让你对金门大桥失望。

description.en: The Marin Headlands are a wild stretch of hillside just north of San Francisco, perfect for photographing or simply admiring the Golden Gate Bridge and the city skyline. This trip covered three viewpoints in order: Point Bonita, Hawk Hill, and Battery Spencer. Each one offers a different angle, and none of them disappoint.
description.zh_cn: 海事岬是旧金山北边的一片荒野高地，非常适合拍摄和眺望金门大桥与旧金山天际线。这次依次去了三个观景点：博尼托角、鹰丘和斯宾塞炮台。每一处的取景角度都各有特色，怎么拍都不会踩雷。

title.en: Would you want to watch this romantic sea of clouds every day?
title.zh_cn: 你会想每天在山顶守着这片浪漫云海吗？

description.en: Mt Tamalpais, just north of San Francisco, is a fantastic spot for watching a sea of clouds roll in. There's a fire lookout tower on the East Peak summit, staffed periodically by fire department volunteers keeping watch for wildfires. As a side effect, they get to watch this romantic cloud sea unfold every single day.
description.zh_cn: 塔玛佩斯山是旧金山以北一处观赏云海的绝佳地点。东峰山顶有一座瞭望塔，消防部门会定期派人上去当"看火人"，监测山火隐患。顺便，他们也就每天都能守着这片浪漫的云海了。

title.en: You hiked ten kilometers and became a lost elk.
title.zh_cn: 你徒步了十公里，变成了一只迷路的麋鹿。

description.en: Tomales Point is a hiking trail at the very northern tip of the Point Reyes peninsula, all open grassy cliffs and howling sea wind. A herd of wild tule elk lives out here, often visible grazing just off the trail. This episode is a pure narrative experiment, telling the whole hike in the second person.
description.zh_cn: 托马利斯角是雷耶斯角半岛最北端的一段徒步路线，沿途是开阔的草地悬崖和呼啸的海风。这里生活着一群野生的图勒麋鹿，常常能在步道两侧远远看到它们的身影。这一期是一次纯粹的叙事实验，用第二人称讲述整段徒步的过程。

title.en: Only here would "Breaking Bad" get used for tourism marketing.
title.zh_cn: 只有这里会用《绝命毒师》做旅游宣传。

description.en: Albuquerque is New Mexico's largest city, and also the real-world setting of "Breaking Bad." Local shops and tourism materials genuinely lean into the show as a selling point, which is a strange thing to see in person. The city itself sits in classic high desert, ringed by volcanic terrain and Indigenous cultural sites.
description.zh_cn: 阿布奎基是新墨西哥州人口最多的城市，也是电视剧《绝命毒师》的故事发生地。当地不少商店和旅游资料真的会拿这部剧当卖点，画风相当特别。城市本身沙漠气候明显，周边环绕着连绵的火山地貌和印第安文化遗迹。

title.en: Today we're visiting a 500-year-old cliffside "big house."
title.zh_cn: 今天要去悬崖上参观五百年前的大房子。

description.en: Bandelier National Monument is an hour's drive from Santa Fe, its canyon walls dotted with cliff dwellings built by ancient Ancestral Puebloans. We mostly hiked the Pueblo Loop Trail and Alcove House Trail, visiting the Tyuonyi Pueblo ruins and the Alcove House site. These carved-cave villages look a lot like Turkey's Cappadocia — New Mexico really does earn its nickname as "the Turkey of North America."
description.zh_cn: 班德利尔国家纪念碑距离新墨西哥州府圣达菲一小时车程，峡谷两侧分布着大量古代普韦布洛人修建的悬崖民居。这次主要走了普韦布洛环线和壁龛之家步道，参观了图约尼遗址和壁龛之家遗址。这种凿岩而居的村庄神似土耳其的卡帕多西亚，新墨西哥不愧是"北美的土耳其"。

title.en: If you don't like adobe architecture, Santa Fe might bore you.
title.zh_cn: 如果你不喜欢 adobe，圣达菲可能会很无聊。

description.en: Santa Fe, New Mexico's state capital, is basically a giant theme park of adobe architecture — nearly the entire city is built in this one style. The tradition traces back to the "great houses" built by local Puebloan peoples, and nearby Taos Pueblo still stands, fully occupied and open to visitors. The first Spanish arrivals, amusingly, were chasing a rumor of a lost "city of gold" here, and found nothing but mud-brick houses instead.
description.zh_cn: 新墨西哥州府圣达菲，简直就是一座大型 adobe（土坯）建筑主题乐园，整座城市几乎清一色是这种风格。这种建筑传统可以追溯到当地普韦布洛原住民修建的"大房子"，附近至今仍有一座持续使用的陶斯大宅可供参观。有意思的是，最早来到这里的西班牙人其实是被骗了——传闻这里有一座"黄金之城"，结果只有满城的土坯房。

title.en: We found America's own "Red Coast Base."
title.zh_cn: 我们找到了美国的"红岸基地"。

description.en: The Very Large Array sits on an empty stretch of grassland west of Socorro in southern New Mexico, with nothing around for miles. The real-world inspiration for the "Red Coast Base" from "The Three-Body Problem" is said to be the Green Bank Telescope in West Virginia, but the VLA belongs to the same broader observation network — and honestly looks even more impressive. Standing out on that plain, it's easy to understand what true isolation actually feels like.
description.zh_cn: 甚大天线阵列位于新墨西哥州南部索科罗以西的荒凉草原上，方圆几十公里几乎荒无人烟。据说《三体》中"红岸基地"的原型其实是西弗吉尼亚的绿岸射电望远镜，但甚大天线阵和绿岸同属一个观测系统，规模看起来还更加壮观。站在这片草原上，真的能体会到什么叫与世隔绝。

title.en: I came to the birthplace of the atomic bomb (it's a bit surreal).
title.zh_cn: 我来到了原子弹的故乡，新墨西哥（有点抽象）。

description.en: This trip to Los Alamos, New Mexico, happened because of the movie "Oppenheimer" — this is the actual birthplace of the atomic bomb and the site of the Manhattan Project. The town itself is fairly quiet, so we mostly spent time at the local science museum, which covers the Manhattan Project in detail. This whole New Mexico trip produced a lot of footage, with more episodes still to come covering White Sands, the Very Large Array, Santa Fe, and beyond.
description.zh_cn: 因为电影《奥本海默》专程去了新墨西哥的洛斯阿拉莫斯，也就是曼哈顿计划的所在地、原子弹真正的故乡。整体而言这里比较平淡，主要逛了当地的科学博物馆，里面详细介绍了曼哈顿计划的历史。这趟新墨西哥之行拍了不少素材，后续还会陆续分享白沙、甚大天线阵列、圣达菲等地的游记。

title.en: I've finally been to Cusco, the place I most wanted to visit a decade ago.
title.zh_cn: 我已经去过了库斯科，十年前最想去的地方。

description.en: This was meant to be the final episode of the Peru series — at one point, even the final episode of the whole channel. But a road trip through New Mexico and Colorado in late May produced way too much footage to just walk away, so the channel goes on. Starting here, future episodes will first drop as a short teaser, with a full version made only if there's enough interest.
description.zh_cn: 这原本是秘鲁系列的最后一期，其实也一度计划是整个频道的收官之作。但五月底去新墨西哥和科罗拉多自驾时又忍不住拍了大量素材，于是频道还会继续下去。从这期开始，之后的视频会先只做一个片头，看反响够不够热烈再决定要不要做完整版。

title.en: Wearing the coolest poncho for the best weekend in Cusco.
title.zh_cn: 穿最帅的斗篷，去库斯科过最精彩的周末。

description.en: This episode follows a weekend of wandering Cusco in a Peruvian poncho, and it turned out far more fun than expected. It's mostly a citywalk, drifting through neighborhoods like San Blas, San Cristóbal, and San Pedro along the way. The highlight was getting pulled into a dance while watching people play instruments in a shop, plus a bowl of "very hygienic" chicken soup noodles from a market stall — tasty, but it hit the stomach hard the next day.
description.zh_cn: 这是一期披着秘鲁斗篷在库斯科闲逛一整个周末的记录，结果比想象中精彩得多。主要以citywalk为主，顺路逛了圣布拉斯、圣克里斯托瓦尔、圣佩德罗等好几个小片区。印象最深的是在乐器店围观时被拉去跳舞，还有市场小摊那碗"干净又卫生"的鸡汤面——味道不错，但第二天肠胃就闹了脾气。

title.en: Behind the rainbow sits the Inca's biggest temple-cum-warehouse.
title.zh_cn: 彩虹的背后，是印加最大的神庙兼超市。

description.en: Raqchi is the largest temple the Inca ever built, an hour and a half from Cusco at the foot of a volcano. Legend says an Inca emperor passing through heard locals describe a great roar here long ago (the volcano erupting) and ordered the biggest temple in the empire built on the spot. Nearby, a row of round, silo-like structures were actually ancient storehouses — together they read like an ancient supermarket — and the temple itself, larger than the one at Machu Picchu, is now reduced to a single standing wall.
description.zh_cn: 拉克奇是印加文明修建过的最大神庙，距离库斯科一个半小时车程，坐落在一座火山脚下。传说印加皇帝路过时听闻此地曾发生过一声巨响（火山爆发），于是下令在此修建这座最大的神庙。旁边一排圆形罐状建筑其实是当年的粮仓，排成一片俨然就是一座古代"超市"，而那座神庙本身比马丘比丘的神庙还大，如今却只剩下一堵墙。

title.en: Pikillacta, a baffling ruin on the outskirts of Cusco.
title.zh_cn: 皮基拉克塔，库斯科郊外一座莫名其妙的遗址。

description.en: Pikillacta is a pre-Inca city ruin about half an hour from Cusco, believed to have served as an administrative center for the Wari civilization in this region. Reliable information about the site is scarce — the one distinctive feature is its unusual double perimeter wall. Wandering around and guessing at what everything was turned out to be genuinely fun, though it's probably not a stop most travelers would care about.
description.zh_cn: 皮基拉克塔是距离库斯科半小时车程的一座前印加城市遗址，据说曾是瓦里文明在这一带的行政中心。关于它的信息其实少之又少，唯一特别的地方是那道双层围墙。逛起来其实挺有意思，只是估计大多数游客对这种冷门遗址兴趣不大。

title.en: On the way to Machu Picchu, don't skip Ollantaytambo.
title.zh_cn: 去马丘比丘的路上，别错过欧雁台。

description.en: Ollantaytambo is an Inca site every traveler passes on the road from Cusco to Machu Picchu. Historically, the Inca made a successful last stand against Spanish cavalry here before retreating with what remained of their forces into Vilcabamba, deep in the jungle — the very place archaeologist Hiram Bingham was actually searching for in 1911 when he stumbled onto Machu Picchu instead. The town's name itself comes from Ollantay, the Inca general who once commanded this fortress.
description.zh_cn: 欧雁台是库斯科前往马丘比丘的路上必经的一座印加遗址。历史上，印加皇帝曾在这里成功击退过西班牙骑兵的进攻，随后率领残部撤入雨林深处的比尔卡班巴——也正是1911年考古学家宾厄姆最初想寻找、结果误打误撞发现马丘比丘的地方。这里的地名也来自当年驻守此地的印加将军奥扬泰。

title.en: The stunning Sacred Valley, tucked between Cusco and Machu Picchu.
title.zh_cn: 绝美印加圣谷，在库斯科与马丘比丘之间。

description.en: This episode is pure Andean scenery — the day we visited the salt pans and the terraces happened to fall right after rain, with the air impossibly clear. It covers the brilliant white Salt Mines of Maras, the concentric terraces of Moray, the Pisac ruins, and the town of Maras itself. The whole Sacred Valley looked especially alive under that particular light.
description.zh_cn: 这一期是纯粹的安第斯风光记录，去马拉斯盐田和莫雷梯田的那天刚好雨过天晴，空气清新得离谱。视频里出现的地方包括雪白的马拉斯盐田、层层叠叠的圆形梯田莫雷，以及皮萨克遗址和马拉斯小镇。整片圣谷（乌鲁班巴河谷）在这样的天气里显得格外生机勃勃。

title.en: Machu Picchu has a "lite" version — with alpacas you can pet freely.
title.zh_cn: 马丘比丘也有"青春版"，这里的羊驼随便撸。

description.en: Pisac is a colonial town about an hour and a half from Cusco, with an Inca ruin of the same name on the mountain behind it — a kind of "lite version" of Machu Picchu. Its terraces are actually bigger and more dramatic than Machu Picchu's, even if the stonework isn't quite as refined. The whole site is huge, and this trip only covered the ruins right near the entrance — the best photo spot, oddly enough, turned out to be right by the restrooms.
description.zh_cn: 皮萨克是距离库斯科一个半小时车程的一座殖民小镇，背后山上有一座同名印加遗址，堪称"马丘比丘青春版"。这里的梯田面积比马丘比丘还要大、还要壮观，只是建筑质量稍逊一筹。整片遗址非常大，这次只逛了门口的一小片区域，实测最佳拍照机位居然就在厕所门口。

title.en: At Sacsayhuamán, I fought altitude sickness to climb rocks and crawl tunnels.
title.zh_cn: 在库斯科山鹰城堡，我顶着高反爬石头钻涵洞。

description.en: Sacsayhuamán is a fortress perched above Cusco, essentially the "close-to-town" version of Machu Picchu. Historically it actually served many of the functions people imagine Machu Picchu did, but because it sat so close to Cusco, the Spanish tore it apart after the Inca used it to besiege them during the city's siege. Even reduced to just its foundation stones, the sheer scale of those boulders is still genuinely impressive.
description.zh_cn: 萨克萨瓦曼城堡俯瞰整个库斯科，可以理解为马丘比丘的"近郊版"。历史上它其实承担了马丘比丘想象中大部分的实际功能，只是因为离库斯科太近，围城战中西班牙人被印加人依托这座堡垒围攻得太狠，事后几乎将它拆得只剩地基。即便只剩巨石地基，这些石块本身的规模也依然十分震撼。

title.en: Cusco's Temple of the Sun — a Spanish courtyard more elegant than Stanford.
title.zh_cn: 库斯科太阳神殿，比斯坦福更优雅的西班牙庭院。

description.en: The Temple of the Sun sits in Cusco, the old Inca capital, and it was once the single most important religious site in the entire empire — more important, even, than Machu Picchu. After the Spanish conquest, they converted the temple directly into a monastery. What's left today is an elegant courtyard, one of the prettiest corners in all of Cusco.
description.zh_cn: 太阳神殿位于秘鲁古都库斯科，曾是整个印加文明最重要的宗教中心，地位甚至比马丘比丘更高。西班牙殖民者入侵之后，直接把神殿改建成了一座修道院。如今这座修道院庭院优雅精致，成了库斯科最漂亮的角落之一。

title.en: 5,000 years old — exploring Caral, the oldest city in the Americas.
title.zh_cn: 五千年历史，探索美洲最古老的城市卡拉尔。

description.en: The Sacred City of Caral sits in the Supe Valley, in the desert north of Lima, a sprawling site left behind by the ancient Norte Chico civilization — likely the oldest city in the Americas. Carbon dating puts it at roughly 5,000 years old, on par with Egypt's Great Pyramids. The mudbrick pyramids here genuinely look their age and don't photograph especially well, but the site upends everything we thought we knew about civilization in the Americas, and like Machu Picchu, it's a UNESCO World Heritage Site well worth the detour.
description.zh_cn: 卡拉尔圣城位于利马以北沙漠中的苏佩河谷，是古代小北文明留下的一片大型遗址群，很可能是整个美洲最古老的城市。碳十四测年显示这里的历史长达五千年，和埃及大金字塔差不多古老。这些土坯金字塔看起来确实饱经沧桑，拍照效果一般，但它彻底改写了我们对美洲文明史的认知，也和马丘比丘一样被列为世界文化遗产，依然值得专程一去。

title.en: Does an overlooked place hide an untold story?
title.zh_cn: 无人问津的地方，会有鲜为人知的故事吗？

description.en: Fortress Paramonga is an obscure Chimú ruin north of Lima that barely gets any visitors. This episode goes looking for whatever lesser-known history might be hiding here. It's not famous, but standing among the mudbrick ruins, you can still sense the scale of what the Chimú once built to guard this stretch of coast.
description.zh_cn: 派拉蒙加堡垒是利马以北一座相当冷门的奇穆文明遗迹，几乎没什么游客专程前来。这次专程去探索了一番，试图挖掘一些鲜为人知的历史细节。虽然名气不大，但站在土坯堡垒的废墟上，依然能感受到当年奇穆人守卫这片海岸的气势。

title.en: A pyramid in Lima's wealthy district, once a dirt hill for motocross.
title.zh_cn: 利马富人区的金字塔，曾是摩托车飙山的土坡。

description.en: Huaca Pucllana sits right in the middle of Miraflores, Lima's wealthiest district, a 1,500-year-old mudbrick pyramid built by the ancient Lima culture. Archaeologists estimate the excavation here still has another twenty years to go. It's strange to think that decades ago, this same ancient monument was just a dirt hill locals used for motocross.
description.zh_cn: 普拉纳金字塔坐落在利马富人区米拉弗洛雷斯的市中心，是一座一千五百年前古利马文明修建的土坯金字塔遗迹。据说这里的考古发掘工作还要持续大约二十年才能完成。很难想象这样一座古老遗迹，几十年前竟然只是当地人骑摩托车飙山的一片土坡。

title.en: Peru's ancient civilizations get weirder and weirder — inside the Larco Museum.
title.zh_cn: 秘鲁古文明一个比一个抽象，沉浸式逛拉科博物馆。

description.en: This was a first attempt at filming a museum visit, and honestly it might be hard to do another one like it. The Larco Museum houses artifacts from Peru's many ancient cultures, each one stranger and more surreal than the last. If a Peru trip is in the works, visiting this museum first — before the actual ruins — makes everything afterward click into place much better.
description.zh_cn: 这是第一次尝试拍摄参观博物馆的视频，拍完感觉以后很难再有类似的题材了。拉科博物馆收藏了秘鲁各个古代文明的文物，一个比一个抽象离奇。如果打算去秘鲁旅行，强烈建议先来这里逛一圈，再去实地看那些遗迹，会更有代入感。

title.en: I jumped off a cliff in Lima... and flew.
title.zh_cn: 我跳下了利马的悬崖，然后飞了起来。

description.en: Lima, Peru's capital, doesn't photograph especially cleanly or prettily, but it feels real — unmistakably Latin American, unmistakably Peruvian. This episode includes paragliding off a coastal cliff, and that first step off the edge is genuinely thrilling. Huaca Pucllana and the Larco Museum, both glimpsed here, each get their own dedicated episode later.
description.zh_cn: 秘鲁首都利马拍出来其实有点脏乱，算不上好看，但胜在真实，很有拉美和秘鲁本地的生活气息。这一期还去了滨海悬崖体验滑翔伞，从崖边一跃而下的瞬间相当刺激。视频里出现的普拉纳金字塔和拉科博物馆之后都会有各自单独的一期。

title.en: Fun fact: you can visit Quebec City without having seen "Goblin."
title.zh_cn: 冷知识：没看过《鬼怪》也能去魁北克城。

description.en: Quebec City, at the mouth of the St. Lawrence River, is arguably the most "European" city in North America, its old town still laid out much the way the French originally built it. Definitely worth a visit — just pick a day with decent light, because the footage from this particular gray day was rough enough that editing it felt like a chore. Even so, it remains one of the easiest places in North America to briefly forget you're not in Europe.
description.zh_cn: 魁北克城坐落在圣劳伦斯河口，是北美最具"欧洲感"的城市，老城区基本保留了法国最初建城时的街道格局，非常值得一逛。唯一的建议是挑一个天气和光线都好的日子去，这次去的那天光线实在太差，一度懒得剪辑素材。整体而言，这里依然是北美最容易让人产生身处欧洲错觉的一座城市。

title.en: France lost the Seven Years' War, leaving North America with only one Paris.
title.zh_cn: 七年战争法国输了，北美只剩一座巴黎。

description.en: This episode on Montreal is a fairly loose collection of scenes, which honestly fits the city itself — there's no single knockout landmark, just a lot of small, distinctly French, distinctly European details. The Biosphere, also seen here, already got its own dedicated episode elsewhere. Turns out filming a crowded, chaotic city well is a lot harder than filming empty wilderness.
description.zh_cn: 这一期关于蒙特利尔的记录内容比较松散，某种程度上蒙特利尔本身也是这样——没有特别惊艳的地标，更多是一些非常法式、非常欧洲的生活细节。这里的生物圈景点已经单独出过一期，就不再重复。事实证明，拍好一座人多嘈杂的城市，真的比拍好荒野难得多。

title.en: Ever seen an island with just one house on it? Here there are a thousand.
title.zh_cn: 你见过只有一座房子的小岛吗？这里有一千座。

description.en: Thousand Islands National Park is a relatively under-the-radar Canadian national park, scattered across the point where Lake Ontario spills into the St. Lawrence River. It's home to a strange, singular kind of waterborne community — plenty of islands are barely big enough to hold a single house. The funnier twist is that most of the islands actually worth seeing sit on the American side of the border.
description.zh_cn: 千岛国家公园是加拿大一个相对小众的国家公园，坐落在安大略湖注入圣劳伦斯河的河口地带，散布着成百上千座小岛。这里形成了一种独特的水上社区文化，很多岛屿小到只够盖一栋房子。更有意思的是，大部分真正值得一看的岛屿其实都在美国境内。

title.en: Missed sunrise in Kamakura? Go square-dance at Tsurugaoka Hachimangu instead.
title.zh_cn: 没在镰仓看到日出，就去鹤冈八幡宫跳广场舞。

description.en: Kamakura is a genuine tourist darling just outside Tokyo, and word was its coastline could produce a view straight out of Hokusai's "The Great Wave off Kanagawa." That was reason enough to leave Tokyo with Old Liu at 3am — only to arrive in Kamakura and find it raining, with nothing visible along the coast at all. With sunrise a bust, the plan shifted to Tsurugaoka Hachimangu, where a round of square dancing with the locals happened instead.
description.zh_cn: 镰仓算是东京周边妥妥的网红城市，听说这里的海边能看到很像《神奈川冲浪里》那样的画面。为此特意凌晨三点就和老刘从东京出发，结果到了镰仓才发现下着雨，海边什么都看不到。既然日出泡汤了，索性转去鹤冈八幡宫，跟当地人一起跳起了广场舞。

title.en: Everything at Nara's Todai-ji is huge — it feels like touring a cathedral.
title.zh_cn: 奈良东大寺的一切都很大，大到像逛一座教堂。

description.en: Todai-ji and nearby Mt. Wakakusa are essentially unmissable stops on any Nara trip. Todai-ji's main hall claims the title of the world's largest wooden structure, and its scale genuinely outdoes Beijing's Imperial Ancestral Temple, which I'd visited before. It's the first Buddhist temple that ever made me feel like I was touring a cathedral instead — cathedrals are famous for going big, temples almost never are.
description.zh_cn: 奈良东大寺和旁边的若草山，应该是奈良旅行绕不开的目的地。东大寺大殿号称"世界最大单体木构建筑"，规模比之前去过的北京太庙还要夸张。它的巨大程度，第一次让人逛佛寺时产生了逛教堂般的感觉——毕竟教堂常以宏大著称，而佛寺很少如此。

title.en: Toshodai-ji, Nara — the Tang-dynasty style left behind by Jianzhen's voyage east.
title.zh_cn: 奈良唐招提寺，鉴真东渡遗留的大唐风骨。

description.en: Toshodai-ji, on Nara's western outskirts, was built under the direction of the monk Jianzhen after his voyage from China, and it's also where he eventually passed away — its layout still broadly reflects Tang-dynasty style. Before architects Liang Sicheng and Lin Huiyin surveyed Shanxi, scholars believed this temple's main hall was one of only two surviving Tang-era wooden structures anywhere. It doesn't draw huge crowds, but it's worth a dedicated visit for anyone interested in the history of cultural exchange between China and Japan.
description.zh_cn: 唐招提寺是奈良西郊的一座寺院，由东渡日本的鉴真和尚主持修建，也是他最终圆寂的地方，整体格局大体保留了唐代的风貌。在梁思成、林徽因考察山西之前，学界一度认为这里的金堂是仅存的两座唐代建筑遗存之一。这里游客不算多，但对每一个对中日文化交流史感兴趣的人来说，都值得专程一看。

title.en: After visiting Kinkaku-ji, I finally read Mishima's "The Temple of the Golden Pavilion."
title.zh_cn: 逛完金阁寺后，我读完了三岛由纪夫的《金阁寺》。

description.en: Kinkaku-ji sits in a somewhat out-of-the-way corner of northwest Kyoto, famous internationally through Yukio Mishima's novel "The Temple of the Golden Pavilion," and back home partly through the anime "Ikkyū-san." The visit happened before reading the novel — the only thing learned on-site was that the pavilion had once burned down and been rebuilt. Reading the book afterward, before editing this footage, it turned out to match the mood of the actual temple remarkably well.
description.zh_cn: 金阁鹿苑寺位于京都西北角，地理位置不算顺路，却因三岛由纪夫的小说《金阁寺》风靡欧美，也曾因动画《聪明的一休》在国内颇有名气。参观当时并没有读过原著，只是在现场听导览提到这座金阁曾经被烧毁重建过。回来剪辑之前补读了这本书，感觉这份文字和鹿苑寺本身的意境异常契合。

title.en: Is Tang-dynasty China still alive in Japan? Kyoto was never Chang'an.
title.zh_cn: 唐朝还在日本？但京都从来不是长安。

description.en: The weather wasn't great that day, and catching up with a college roommate not seen in three years took priority over careful filming. There was a genuine hope of finding traces of ancient Chang'an in Kyoto, but Kyoto is simply Kyoto — at most the scale of a small county town, never anything close to Chang'an. Toshodai-ji in Nara the next day carried far more of that old Tang-dynasty feel — Kyoto is plenty interesting on its own, just don't go looking for Chang'an there.
description.zh_cn: 那天天气不太好，加上和三年没见的大学室友聊得太投入，索性没怎么认真拍。原本是想在京都找一找长安的影子，但事实证明京都终究是京都，规模顶多算一座县城，从来都不曾是长安。反倒是第二天在奈良看到的唐招提寺，唐风气质更浓一些——京都本身很有趣，只是别指望在这里找到长安。

title.en: Visiting a Song-dynasty-era cliff "palace" in the snow — except it's in Colorado.
title.zh_cn: 雪中拜访一座宋代悬崖"宫殿"，只是它在科罗拉多。

description.en: Cliff Palace sits in Mesa Verde National Park, Colorado, the largest cliff dwelling in the United States, built by the ancestors of the Southwest's Puebloan peoples. Construction dates to roughly China's Southern Song dynasty, and by a strange coincidence, the site was abandoned the very same year Möngke Khan died at Diaoyu Fortress and Kublai Khan became Great Khan. Mesa Verde earned its UNESCO World Heritage status largely because of dwellings just like this one.
description.zh_cn: 悬崖宫殿位于科罗拉多州梅萨维德国家公园，是美国规模最大的悬崖住宅遗迹，由美国西南原住民普韦布洛人的先祖建造。它建于中国南宋时期，巧合的是，蒙哥死于钓鱼城、忽必烈称帝的那一年，这座大宅恰好也被废弃。梅萨维德国家公园正是因为这些壮观的悬崖住宅遗址而被列入世界文化遗产。

title.en: America has its own Machu Picchu, and it's in New Mexico.
title.zh_cn: 美国也有自己的马丘比丘，就在新墨西哥。

description.en: Pueblo Bonito, in Chaco Culture National Historical Park in northwestern New Mexico, is the largest surviving Indigenous American ruin in the country, a masterwork of the ancestral Puebloan people. Its scale genuinely rivals Machu Picchu, and it predates it by roughly three hundred years. The harsh local environment kept its architecture from matching Maya achievements, but its uniqueness and craftsmanship still make it well worth seeing — one of the few UNESCO World Heritage Sites in the US.
description.zh_cn: 帕布罗·博尼托遗址位于新墨西哥州西北部的查科文化国家历史公园，是美国现存规模最大的原住民遗迹，普韦布洛人的杰作。它的规模足以与马丘比丘相提并论，而年代还要比马丘比丘早上三百年。受当地严酷自然环境所限，建筑成就比不上玛雅遗迹，但独特性和精美程度依然十分值得一看，也是美国为数不多的世界文化遗产之一。

title.en: Nowadays, you have to pay your own way into San Francisco's prison.
title.zh_cn: 现在进旧金山的监狱，得自己掏钱了。

description.en: Alcatraz sits between San Francisco and the Golden Gate Bridge, with a jaw-dropping view of both bridges and the city — and Americans decided the best use for it was a prison. That's all fifty years in the past now, though; today, "getting into prison" means buying a ticket, since it's been turned into a tourist attraction. It's shown up in countless films, games, and TV shows, and apparently the inmates, with nothing else to do, got through an unusual amount of philosophy that most people would never finish.
description.zh_cn: 恶魔岛坐落在旧金山和金门大桥之间，坐拥双桥一城的绝佳景观，却被美国人挑来当作监狱使用。当然那都是五十年前的事了，如今想"进监狱"得自己花钱买票，因为它早已被改造成一处景点。这里也是无数电影、游戏和电视剧的取景地，据说当年的囚犯因为无事可做，反而读了大量常人根本读不下去的哲学书。

title.en: Civ 6 Pilgrimage: what's the real significance of the Delicate Arch?
title.zh_cn: 文明6圣地巡礼：精致拱门，到底有何深意？

description.en: This is a new entry in the long-running "Civ 6 Pilgrimage" series, this time centered on the natural wonder Delicate Arch. Arches National Park turns out to be a small but polished park, and all three areas covered here — Delicate Arch, Double O and Window Arches, and Devils Garden — were well worth the visit. This series had nearly gone dormant due to a busy work schedule, until a rainy day suddenly brought back the urge to edit.
description.zh_cn: 这是"文明6圣地巡礼"系列时隔许久之后的新一期，主角是自然奇观"精致拱门"。拱门国家公园其实是一座小而精致的公园，这次走过的三片区域——精致拱门、双零拱门与窗户拱门、以及魔鬼花园——都相当值得一看。工作繁忙一度让这个系列几乎要断更，恰好某天外面下雨，突然又有了剪辑的冲动。

title.en: Civ 6 Pilgrimage: Crater Lake, said to be the most beautiful view on Earth.
title.zh_cn: 文明6圣地巡礼：火山口湖，地球上最美的景观？

description.en: Crater Lake sits in southern Oregon, a kind of North American answer to Heaven Lake atop Changbai Mountain, only considerably larger. It's not one of the more commonly picked natural wonders in Civilization 6, so a lot of players probably don't remember it well. Jack London genuinely visited Crater Lake once, and plenty of tourism materials still quote his praise of it today.
description.zh_cn: 火山口湖位于美国俄勒冈州南部，某种程度上相当于北美版的长白山天池，只不过面积要大得多。它在文明6的自然奇观列表里不算特别常见，很多玩家可能都没什么印象。作家杰克·伦敦确实到访过这里，如今不少宣传材料也依然在引用他当年的赞美之词。

title.en: Civ 6 Pilgrimage: Machu Picchu, a sky city wrapped in cloud.
title.zh_cn: 文明6圣地巡礼：云雾环绕的天空之城马丘比丘。

description.en: Machu Picchu is believed to have been built as a private retreat for Pachacuti, the founding emperor of the Inca Empire, hidden deep in the cloud forest of the Peruvian Andes — actually getting there takes real effort. Because the Spanish never discovered it, the site survived largely intact, but that also means almost nothing was ever written down about it. All that's left to see now is stone, in a setting whose visual impact is stunning even if, technically speaking, the architecture wasn't the most advanced of its era.
description.zh_cn: 马丘比丘据信是印加开国皇帝帕查库特克下令修建的一座"避暑山庄"，深藏在秘鲁安第斯高原的云雾雨林之中，专程打卡相当折腾。因为西班牙殖民者从未发现这里，它得以相对完整地保存下来，但也因此几乎没有留下任何历史文字记载。如今能看到的只剩下满地的石头，选址堪称绝妙、视觉效果极为震撼，只是论建筑技术，它并非那个时代世界上最先进的水平。

title.en: Civ 6 City-State Pilgrimage: braving chaos to see the Nazca Lines.
title.zh_cn: 文明6城邦巡礼：穿过骚乱去看纳斯卡地画。

description.en: This is a travel record from Nazca, in southern Peru. In Civilization 6, the Nazca city-state uses that iconic spider as its icon, and its unique improvement, the Nazca Lines, is basically the perfect fix for a flat, empty desert tile. Beyond the small-plane flight over the lines themselves, this trip also covered the Antonini Museum, Nazca's ancient aqueducts, and an Inca palace ruin — genuinely worthwhile for anyone drawn to mysterious, quietly charming ancient cultures like this one.
description.zh_cn: 这是在秘鲁南部纳斯卡的一段旅行记录。文明6里的纳斯卡城邦以那只标志性的蜘蛛图案作为图标，其专属设施"纳斯卡地画"堪称拯救平坦沙漠地块的利器。除了坐小飞机看地画，这次还参观了安东尼尼博物馆、纳斯卡坎儿井以及一处印加宫殿遗址，对神秘又可爱的古纳斯卡文化感兴趣的话，这里绝对值得一逛。

title.en: Civ 6 Pilgrimage: the Statue of Liberty, quietly holding the Chinese Exclusion Act.
title.zh_cn: 文明6圣地巡礼：手拿《排华法案》的自由女神像。

description.en: This visit to the Statue of Liberty happened during a full week in New York in the summer of 2023. Digging into the history afterward turned up an interesting overlap: the statue was completed in 1886, right in the middle of America's third great wave of immigration (1880–1914), much of which passed through Ellis Island right beside it. At the very same time, the notorious Chinese Exclusion Act (1882–1943) shut Chinese immigrants out of that wave entirely — so, figuratively speaking, the statue's other hand has always been holding that act too, even though in reality it holds something else entirely.
description.zh_cn: 这是2023年夏天在纽约待了整整一周期间专程去看的自由女神像。查资料时发现了一个很有意思的时间线：自由女神像建成于1886年，恰好落在美国第三次移民浪潮（1880-1914年）之中，大批移民正是经由自由女神像所在的埃利斯岛登陆上岸。而与此同时生效的《排华法案》（1882-1943年）却把华人完全排除在这波移民浪潮之外——所以说，自由女神像的另一只手，其实"握着"的正是这份法案，尽管现实中她左手拿的另有其物。

title.en: Civ 6 Pilgrimage: Yosemite, a giant granite playground.
title.zh_cn: 文明6圣地巡礼：花岗岩构筑的巨型游乐园优胜美地。

description.en: This marks the first real, proper visit to Yosemite National Park. As California's oldest and most famous national park, it fully lives up to the hype. It's easy to see why it earned a spot as a natural wonder in Civilization 6.
description.zh_cn: 这是第一次真正意义上好好游览优胜美地国家公园的记录。作为加州最早也最负盛名的国家公园，它确实名不虚传。也难怪它能在文明6里被选为自然奇观之一。

title.en: Civ 6 Pilgrimage: watching sunrise with the Golden Gate Bridge on my 25th birthday.
title.zh_cn: 文明6圣地巡礼：25岁生日，和金门大桥一起看日出。

description.en: This footage was actually shot on the spring equinox, but somehow didn't get edited and posted until after the fall equinox rolled around. Since it was a workday, the plan was an early start and a drive up to Slacker Hill, a high point north of the Golden Gate Bridge, to catch sunrise — expectations weren't particularly high going in. That sunrise ended up far exceeding them, especially the golden fog rolling over the bridge and the whole hillside, which looked absolutely stunning.
description.zh_cn: 这段视频其实拍摄于春分那天，结果拖到秋分之后才剪出来发布。因为是工作日，那天起了个大早，开车去金门大桥北边的高点 Slacker Hill 等日出，本来没抱太大期待。结果那天的日出大大超出预期，尤其是笼罩着金门大桥和整片山坡的金色薄雾，美得让人挪不开眼。

title.en: Civ 6 Pilgrimage: after building this wonder, Montreal basically went bankrupt.
title.zh_cn: 文明6圣地巡礼：敲完奇观，蒙特利尔就破产了。

description.en: Strictly speaking, it was the 1976 Olympics that actually bankrupted Montreal, not the 1967 World's Fair. But the roots of the decline trace back to a speech by the French prime minister during that same World's Fair, which helped fuel Quebec's separatist movement and, down the line, dragged on Montreal's economy. So rounding generously, "build this wonder, and Montreal goes bankrupt" isn't too far from the truth.
description.zh_cn: 严格来说，真正让蒙特利尔破产的是1976年的奥运会，而不是1967年的世博会。但一系列衰落的根源，其实要追溯到世博会期间法国总理的一番发言，间接引发了魁北克的部分独立运动，进而拖累了蒙特利尔的经济。所以四舍五入来说，"敲完这个奇观之后，蒙特利尔就破产了"倒也不算太离谱。

title.en: The Biosphere 2 from your textbook was actually a weight-loss retreat?
title.zh_cn: 课本上的生物圈二号，其实是个减肥计划？

description.en: Biosphere 2 sits less than an hour outside Tucson, Arizona, the site of a real 1990s experiment in artificial, sealed ecosystems. Middle school biology textbooks used to mention this place when covering the concept of a biosphere; today it's run as a research lab by the University of Arizona and open to visitors. The original design honestly feels pretty impractical, more like a weight-loss retreat dressed up as a luxury resort — the living quarters are genuinely nice, nice enough to make you want to move in.
description.zh_cn: 生物圈二号位于亚利桑那州第二大城市图森郊外，不到一小时车程，是1990年代人造生物圈实验的旧址。初中生物课本讲到生物圈概念时曾提到过这里，如今它归亚利桑那大学管理，作为实验室对外开放参观。整体设计其实相当不合理，更像是一个豪华度假村里的减肥计划——住宿条件是真的好，好到让人也想住进去体验一把。

title.en: Civ 6 Pilgrimage: a tsunami destroyed Kotoku-in, exposing the Kamakura Buddha ever since.
title.zh_cn: 文明6圣地巡礼：海啸冲毁高德院，大佛直面人间。

description.en: Kotoku-in is a tiny temple in Kamakura, famous almost entirely because of the Great Buddha of Kamakura sitting in its courtyard. Whether it's from playing Civilization 6, reading Kipling, or seeing Kawase Hasui's woodblock prints, a lot of people have already "met" this statue in some form. The hall that once sheltered it was reportedly destroyed by a tsunami long ago, and the Buddha has sat out in the open ever since.
description.zh_cn: 高德院坐落在日本镰仓，本身是一座很小很小的佛寺，却因为院内那尊著名的镰仓大佛而广为人知。不管是玩过文明6，读过吉卜林的作品，还是见过川濑巴水的浮世绘，很多人多半都已经"见过"这尊大佛。据说曾经罩住大佛的殿堂在历史上被海啸冲毁，从此这尊佛像便一直露天矗立至今。

title.en: Remember to find this heart-shaped rock in the desert.
title.zh_cn: 记得去沙漠里找这颗心。

description.en: Joshua Tree National Park, right next to LA, hides a startlingly realistic heart-shaped rock. It's a great spot for photos and meme-worthy poses alike. The real fun, though, is the hunt through the boulder field to actually find it.
description.zh_cn: 洛杉矶隔壁的约书亚树国家公园里，藏着一颗形状极其逼真的心形石头。这里很适合拍照，也很适合摆拍各种表情包。不过真正好玩的部分，其实是在乱石堆里寻找它的过程。

title.en: Joshua trees are boring — all they do is dance.
title.zh_cn: 约书亚树真的很无聊，他们只会跳舞。

description.en: Joshua Tree National Park sits right next to LA, home to a huge crowd of oddly-shaped Joshua trees. Their twisted branches make them look permanently frozen mid-dance. It's a desert park close enough to the city, yet it feels like an entirely different world.
description.zh_cn: 约书亚树国家公园就在洛杉矶隔壁，园内生活着一大群造型各异的约书亚树。它们枝干弯曲扭转，看上去就像永远定格在跳舞的瞬间。这片沙漠公园离城市很近，却完全是另一个世界。

title.en: Six firsts in my life, all in Channel Islands National Park.
title.zh_cn: 人生的六个第一次，都在海峡群岛国家公园。

description.en: Channel Islands National Park is a wonderfully under-the-radar park off the coast of LA, with dolphins, whales, and sharks all sharing this stretch of channel. The islands are also home to a rare native fox, earning the park the nickname "North America's Galápagos." One more fun fact: the world's very first offshore oil rig was built right here in this channel.
description.zh_cn: 海峡群岛国家公园是洛杉矶外海一处超级小众的国家公园，海豚、鲸鱼、鲨鱼在这片海峡里应有尽有。岛上还生活着稀有的小狐狸，因此被称为"北美的加拉帕戈斯"。更有趣的是，世界上第一台海上石油钻井平台，就诞生在这片海峡里。

title.en: Caving in California, exploring four lava tubes with an iPhone 17.
title.zh_cn: 加州探洞：带着iPhone17钻了四个火山洞。

description.en: This trip covered four developed lava tubes in Lava Beds National Monument, in Northern California: Mushpot, Skull, Merrill, and Boulevard Cave. It rained the entire day, so the surface scenery wasn't much to look at, but caving doesn't care about weather. The caves themselves were dead quiet, and the sound of rain outside turned out more interesting than any soundtrack, so this one got no music at all.
description.zh_cn: 这一期去了加州北部的岩浆床国家纪念碑，钻了四个开发好的家养熔岩洞：Mushpot、Skull、Merrill 和 Boulevard Cave。当天赶上下雨，地面风景不算好看，好在钻洞本身完全不受天气影响。洞里格外安静，雨声比任何背景音乐都更有意思，索性就没加任何配乐。

title.en: Ever seen an upside-down stalactite? It's a fossilized hot spring.
title.zh_cn: 你见过倒立的钟乳石吗？是温泉的化石。

description.en: This is a visit to the South Tufa area on Mono Lake's south shore, right off Highway 395 in California. These pillars look a little goofy at first glance, but they're actually "cement" columns built up from calcium carbonate spewed by hot springs deep underwater. You could think of them as fossilized hot springs — or, just as accurately, upside-down stalactites.
description.zh_cn: 这是在加州395公路旁莫诺湖南岸探访石灰华（South Tufa）的记录。这些石头柱子乍看丑萌丑萌的，其实是水底温泉喷涌出的碳酸钙层层堆积形成的"水泥"柱。换句话说，可以把它们看成是"温泉的化石"，或者说"倒立的钟乳石"。

title.en: So-so fall color, gorgeous reflections — pretending autumn happened this year too.
title.zh_cn: 潦草的秋色和绝美的倒影，假装今年也来过秋天。

description.en: California's Highway 395 is one of the state's best-known fall foliage drives, passing lake after lake along the way. This trip cut through Yosemite National Park with a stop at Tenaya Lake, passed Mono Lake on the way to Silver Lake in the June Lake Loop, and raced the sunset to reach Lake Sabrina near Bishop. The lakes and mountains delivered — the fall color, admittedly, still couldn't quite compete with Cornell.
description.zh_cn: 加州395公路是州内颇负盛名的赏秋公路，沿途大大小小的湖泊数不胜数。这次途经优胜美地国家公园，在特纳亚湖短暂停留，随后路过莫诺湖，去了六月湖环线里的银湖，最后赶在日落前抵达毕晓普附近的萨布丽娜湖。湖景和山景都相当精彩，只是这里的秋色，终究比不上康奈尔大学。

title.en: Where California's first gold nugget was discovered.
title.zh_cn: 加州第一块黄金被发现的地方。

description.en: Coloma is a ghost town on the south side of scenic Lake Tahoe, though 150 years ago it was the single destination people worldwide were racing toward, after gold was found in the South Fork American River running through it. California hadn't yet become a state at the time, and in that brief legal vacuum, gold hunters simply kept whatever they dug up. Within a decade the population exploded and California became a US state — in a real sense, this is where the state was actually born.
description.zh_cn: 科洛马是一座位于太浩湖南侧的鬼城，一百五十年前却曾是全世界人趋之若鹜的目的地——因为有人在流经这里的美国河发现了黄金。彼时加州尚未建州，恰好出现一段法律真空期，淘金者只要跑到这里，挖出来的黄金就归自己所有。短短十年间加州人口暴涨，迅速建州，某种意义上，这里正是加州真正诞生的地方。

title.en: Wait, Russia had a colony in California?
title.zh_cn: 俄罗斯居然殖民过加州？

description.en: Who would have guessed Russia once ran a colony in California? Fort Ross is a historic fort on the Sonoma coast, in the San Francisco Bay Area. It once marked the southernmost outpost of Russian activity anywhere in the Americas.
description.zh_cn: 谁能想到俄罗斯人居然殖民过加州？罗斯堡是旧金山湾区索诺马海岸线上的一座历史堡垒。它曾是俄罗斯人在整个美洲活动范围最靠南的据点。

title.en: Maybe you're secretly a giant saguaro cactus.
title.zh_cn: 也许你是一棵巨型仙人柱？

description.en: Saguaro National Park sits just outside Tucson, Arizona, starring a desert full of giant saguaro cacti in every strange shape imaginable. Most of them are centuries old, and no two look quite alike. Walking through the desert here feels a bit like wandering a quiet forest of green sculptures.
description.zh_cn: 巨人柱国家公园位于亚利桑那州图森，主角是沙漠里那些造型各异的巨型仙人柱。它们大多有着几百年的历史，形态千奇百怪，好看又有趣。走在这片沙漠里，仿佛置身于一片安静的绿色雕塑森林。

title.en: We climbed the giant dunes under snowy peaks at sunset.
title.zh_cn: 日落时分，我们爬上了雪山下的大沙丘。

description.en: Great Sand Dunes National Park has a simple pitch — a genuinely massive field of sand dunes, though finding them in Colorado does feel a little out of place. Climbing them turned out to be exhausting, and finding a patch nobody had walked on yet was even harder. The payoff was a backdrop of snow-capped peaks that made the whole climb worth it.
description.zh_cn: 大沙丘国家公园的看点很直接，就是一片巨大的沙丘，只是它出现在科罗拉多多少有点违和感。爬上沙丘是真的累人，想找到一片完全没被踩过的地方更是难上加难。好在雪山作背景的画面，足以弥补这一路的辛苦。

title.en: A first-time hiker conquers their first "Chief."
title.zh_cn: 徒步小白，征服人生第一位"酋长"。

description.en: This is a full record of hiking Stawamus Chief, a well-known trail near Vancouver, in late April — also the first attempt at documenting a hike in this much detail. The real takeaway was realizing how much more preparation real long-distance hiking demands: better cardio, better fitness, and a backpack actually built for the trail. Food and water need real planning too — a bag of gummies and one bottle of water turned out to be nowhere near enough.
description.zh_cn: 这是四月底徒步温哥华附近著名的斯塔瓦姆斯酋长岩的全记录，也是第一次尝试做得这么详细的一期。真正的收获是意识到长线徒步需要更多准备——有氧和体能得练起来，背包也得换成真正适合徒步的款式。能量补给和饮用水更要提前规划好，一包软糖加一瓶水显然是远远不够的。

title.en: Yogurt-colored water runs through these waterfalls.
title.zh_cn: 酸奶般的水，修好了这几座瀑布。

description.en: This episode covers three waterfalls explored across Yoho and Banff National Parks: Takakkaw Falls, Natural Bridge, and Mistaya Canyon. All three share one thing in common — water the color of yogurt, a milky, almost opaque white. It's a visually striking quirk, and a distinctive trait of glacial meltwater in the Canadian Rockies.
description.zh_cn: 这一期记录了在幽鹤国家公园和班夫国家公园探索的三处瀑布：塔喀可桂瀑布、天然桥和迷死他呀峡谷。三处的共同特点，是水流都带着一种酸奶般的乳白色泽。视觉效果格外有趣，也是加拿大落基山脉冰川融水的一大特色。

title.en: Help me pick a better name for Canada's mini Kanas Lake.
title.zh_cn: 帮加拿大的迷你喀纳斯湖选个新名字吧。

description.en: Emerald Lake is the most accessible lake in Canada's Yoho National Park, and pretty much the only "public" one, while the rest of the truly gorgeous spots stay hidden away. Its scenery rivals China's Kanas Lake, yet it's stuck with about the most generic name imaginable — you'd think it was somewhere in Myanmar. This episode isn't the most polished either, since it was actually filmed earlier than the others, before there was much of a plan for how to shoot it.
description.zh_cn: 翡翠湖是加拿大幽鹤国家公园里最容易到达的一座湖，也是几乎唯一"公开"的一个，剩下真正好看的地方大多养在深闺无人识。它的山水景致丝毫不输中国的喀纳斯湖，偏偏顶着一个烂大街的名字，让人误以为是在缅甸。这一期拍得不算完美——去的时间其实早于后面几期，当时既没准备好，也没什么拍摄思路。

title.en: Finally tasted glacier ice for the first time — and not in Iceland.
title.zh_cn: 吃上了人生第一口冰川，竟然不在冰岛。

description.en: Getting up close to a real glacier had been on the list for a while, and it turned out Jasper National Park, near Banff, has one — the Athabasca Glacier — sitting right off the highway, with a tour that lets you actually walk onto the ice. The glacier itself isn't especially photogenic, and there's not much to do once you're up there; they don't even let you near the ice towers. Still, it counts as tasting glacier ice for the first time — though drinking the meltwater is probably not the best idea, hygiene-wise.
description.zh_cn: 一直想找机会近距离接触冰川，这次在班夫附近发现贾斯珀国家公园有一条离公路特别近的冰川——阿萨巴斯卡冰川，还有旅游项目能直接走上冰川，于是果断安排。冰川本身颜值一般，能做的事情也不多，冰塔林更是不让靠近。不过好歹是吃上了人生第一口冰川，至于喝冰川水嘛，卫生状况就不太好说了。

title.en: Summer in Banff is comfortable enough to make you want to settle down there.
title.zh_cn: 夏天的班夫，舒服到让人想在这结婚生娃。

description.en: This is a first-impressions record of Banff National Park. Day one skipped the biggest tourist hotspots entirely, favoring a handful of quietly beautiful spots instead — especially an unnamed little lake early on, peaceful enough to make you consider just settling down and raising a family right there. The day also covered Banff townsite itself, the Sulphur Mountain gondola, Cascade Falls, the Three Sisters viewpoint, and the Hoodoos.
description.zh_cn: 这是对加拿大班夫国家公园的第一印象记录。第一天没有直奔任何热门打卡点，反而去了几处格外舒服的地方，尤其是一开始遇到的那片无名小湖，静谧得让人想着要不要干脆在这里成家。这一天走过的地方还包括班夫小镇本身、硫磺山缆车、喀斯喀特瀑布、"三姐妹"山峰和蘑菇观景点。

title.en: If you like cats, you'll like "Cat Paw Lake."
title.zh_cn: 喜欢猫的话，你会喜欢"猫爪湖"。

description.en: Peyto Lake is a genuinely famous, extremely photogenic lake in Banff National Park. Getting down to the actual shoreline is difficult, but from the viewpoint above, the lake's outline looks exactly like a cat's paw — a guaranteed great photo. Beyond snapping that one shot, though, there's honestly not much else to do here.
description.zh_cn: 佩托湖是加拿大班夫国家公园里一座相当出名的网红湖。从湖边很难真正走到水边，但站在高处的观景台俯瞰，湖泊的轮廓看上去就像一只猫爪，出片效果拉满。除了拍照留念，这里其实也没有太多别的可玩的了。

title.en: Izu has no dancing girls, but Mt Fuji will dance for you instead.
title.zh_cn: 伊豆没有舞女，但富士山能为你跳舞。

description.en: Mt. Omuro is the Izu Peninsula's famous "matcha cake" mountain, its slopes burned in an annual ceremony that briefly turns them the color of chocolate. As a small volcanic cone, it sits in a great spot, and the view of Mt. Fuji from the summit is excellent too. It's the kind of place worth visiting often if you lived in Tokyo — unfortunately, that's not the case here.
description.zh_cn: 大室山是伊豆半岛著名的"抹茶蛋糕山"，每年都会举办烧山仪式，把满山的抹茶色瞬间烧成巧克力色。作为一座小型火山锥，它的地理位置极佳，站在山顶远眺富士山的视野也相当不错。如果住在东京，这大概会是一个想经常回访的地方，可惜现实中并不住在那里。

title.en: Next time you visit Izu Panorama Park, bring some love along.
title.zh_cn: 下次来伊豆全景公园，记得带上爱情。

description.en: This is a plain, unembellished record of Izu Panorama Park — no fitting soundtrack ever turned up (much like love itself), so this one just runs without music. If the park is worth visiting at all, it's probably only in autumn. Any other season, the ¥2,500 ticket feels a bit steep, and honestly not many people are likely dying to come.
description.zh_cn: 这是一段伊豆全景公园的流水账记录，因为一直没找到合适的背景音乐（就像没能找到爱情一样），干脆就没配乐。这里如果真值得一去，大概也只有秋天。其他季节2500日元的门票感觉略贵，估计也没多少人真的想专程来看。

title.en: I want to be a swan at the foot of Mt Fuji.
title.zh_cn: 我想在富士山脚下当一只天鹅。

description.en: Feeding swans at Hirano-no-hama, on the shore of Lake Yamanaka, might be the best possible way to experience Mt. Fuji. The lake sits calm in the early morning, swans drift right up close, and Fuji looms faintly in the background the whole time. Given the choice, becoming a swan at the foot of Mt. Fuji here doesn't sound like a bad deal at all.
description.zh_cn: 在富士山山中湖畔的平野之滨喂天鹅，大概是打开富士山的最佳方式之一。清晨的湖面平静，天鹅成群靠近，富士山就在不远处的背景里若隐若现。如果可以选择，也想在这里当一只富士山脚下的天鹅。
